tagline: Safe home magic missions for kids
secondaryUse: Ages 4-6 can use selected starter missions with a grown-up.
privacyStance: No login, no ads, no analytics, no child profiles, no open text fields, no camera, no microphone, no location.

missions.title: The Flower That Wakes Up
missions.subtitle: A paper flower opens by itself on water.
safety: A grown-up cuts the paper flower for younger kids.
Keep water on a towel or tray.
Do not drink craft water.
missions.effect: A folded paper flower slowly opens like magic when it touches water.
steps: Draw a simple flower on paper.
Cut it out. Younger kids ask a grown-up to cut.
Fold each petal toward the center.
Place the folded flower gently on water.
Say your magic word and watch it open.
missions.secret: Paper fibers soak up water and relax, which makes the folded petals open.
missions.showTip: Tell the audience the flower is sleeping. Tap the water and say, 'Wake up, tiny flower!'

before: Do you have paper and a shallow plate of water?
Is a grown-up helping with scissors?
Did you put a towel under the plate?
during: What color is your magic flower?
What magic word will wake it up?
Ready to place it on the water gently?
after: Was it slow, medium, or fast?
Want to try a bigger or smaller flower?

missions.title: The Bendy Pencil
missions.subtitle: Make a pencil look rubbery without bending it.
safety: Hold the pencil away from faces.
Do not poke people or pets.
missions.effect: A straight pencil looks like it bends and wiggles.
steps: Hold the pencil lightly near one end.
Keep your hand loose.
Move your hand up and down in small fast motions.
Show it from the side so it looks bendy.
Stop and let everyone see the pencil is still straight.
missions.secret: Your eyes blend the fast motion into a bendy shape.
missions.showTip: Say, 'This pencil is tired of being straight!' Then wiggle it for only a few seconds.

before: Do you have a pencil or crayon?
Can you stand where everyone sees the side?
during: Are you holding it loosely?
Can you wiggle smaller and faster?

missions.title: The Shadow Rabbit
missions.subtitle: Turn your hands into a wall shadow animal.
safety: A grown-up handles the lamp or phone flashlight for younger kids.
Do not shine light into eyes.
missions.effect: Your hands create a rabbit shadow that appears on the wall.
steps: Point a light at a blank wall.
Put your hands between the light and wall.
Cross your thumbs to make a head shape.
Raise two fingers for ears.
Move one finger to make the rabbit twitch.
missions.secret: Objects block light. Your hands make the shape that appears on the wall.
missions.showTip: Tell a tiny story: 'A rabbit jumped into the room but only brought its shadow.'

before: Is the light pointed at the wall, not eyes?
Can you see your hand shadow?
during: Can you make two ears?
Can the rabbit wave?
after: Did your shadow look like a rabbit?

missions.title: Crayon Mind Reader
missions.subtitle: Guess a crayon color with a secret peek.
safety: Do not put crayons near mouths or noses.
Keep crayons off walls and furniture.
missions.effect: A spectator secretly chooses a crayon, and you correctly name the color.
steps: Turn your back and ask someone to place one crayon in your hand.
Hold the crayon behind your back.
Lightly scrape a tiny bit of color onto your thumbnail.
Return the crayon behind your back and turn around.
Pretend to think, glance at your thumbnail, and name the color.
missions.secret: The color mark on your thumbnail tells you the answer.
missions.showTip: Do not look too quickly. Ask, 'Are you thinking of the color very loudly?' then reveal it.

before: Do you have crayons?
Can you keep the secret scrape tiny?
during: Did you keep the crayon behind your back?
Can you glance without making it obvious?

missions.title: Pepper Runs Away
missions.subtitle: Make pepper scatter with one soapy touch.
safety: Use only a tiny bit of soap.
Do not touch eyes after touching soap or pepper.
Wash hands after the trick.
Keep the plate on a towel.
missions.effect: Pepper floating on water zooms away when the magic finger touches the center.
steps: Pour a little water into a plate.
Sprinkle pepper across the water.
Put a tiny dot of dish soap on a cotton swab or fingertip.
Touch the center of the water.
Watch the pepper race away.
missions.secret: Soap changes the water surface and pushes the pepper outward.
missions.showTip: Say, 'Pepper, make a circle!' Touch the water only once for a clean surprise.

before: Is a grown-up nearby?
Do you have a towel under the plate?
Did you use only a tiny dot of soap?
during: Is the pepper floating?
Ready to touch the center once?
after: What happened when you touched the water?

missions.title: Jumping Paper Clips
missions.subtitle: Two clips jump and link when paper is pulled.
items: long strip of paper or dollar bill
safety: Paper clips are small. Keep them away from mouths and younger siblings.
Use a paper strip instead of money if preferred.
missions.effect: Two paper clips jump off the paper and link together.
steps: Fold the paper strip into an S shape with three panels.
Clip the first two layers together near one side.
Clip the last two layers together near the other side.
Hold both ends of the paper.
Pull the ends apart quickly and the clips jump together.
missions.secret: The folds line the clips up so pulling the paper forces them to snap together.
missions.showTip: Count down: 'Three, two, one—jump!' Pull cleanly and let the clips land on the table.

before: Do you have two paper clips?
Are small objects away from little kids?
Did you fold the paper into three panels?
during: Are the clips on different sides of the fold?
Ready to pull both ends?

missions.title: Invisible Salt
missions.subtitle: Make a pinch of salt disappear from a napkin.
safety: Use only a tiny pinch of salt.
Do not eat salt from the trick.
Wipe the table after.
missions.effect: Salt seems to vanish after being wrapped in a napkin.
steps: Place a tiny pinch of salt in the center of a napkin.
Fold the napkin once, secretly letting the salt slide into a corner pocket.
Pretend the salt is in the middle.
Tap the middle and say the magic word.
Open the middle to show it empty, keeping the hidden corner folded.
missions.secret: The salt is hidden in a folded corner, not in the center where the audience looks.
missions.showTip: Keep the napkin moving naturally. Do not over-explain where the salt is.

before: Do you have a napkin and tiny pinch of salt?
Is the table dry?
during: Did the salt slide into the secret corner?
Can you keep that corner folded?

missions.title: The Number 9 Spell
missions.subtitle: A math trick that always finds 9.
safety: No physical materials needed.
missions.effect: Someone picks a number, but the answer always becomes 9.
steps: Ask someone to pick a number from 1 to 9.
Tell them to multiply it by 9.
If the answer has two digits, add those digits together.
Tell them to think of the final number.
Reveal: 'Your number is 9!'
missions.secret: Multiples of 9 from 9 to 81 have digits that add to 9.
missions.showTip: Write a big hidden 9 on paper before the trick, then reveal your prediction.

before: Does your helper know multiplication by 9?
Want to use paper for the math?
during: Did your helper multiply by 9?
Did they add the digits?
after: Want to learn why it works?

missions.title: Coin Portal Cup
missions.subtitle: A coin seems to appear under a cup.
items: paper matching the table or a second coin cover
safety: Coins are small. Keep away from mouths and younger children.
Use a plastic or paper cup, not glass.
missions.effect: A coin disappears from view and seems to travel under a cup.
steps: Before the show, hide a coin under the cup or cover the cup opening with matching paper.
Place another coin on the table.
Cover the visible coin with the cup.
Move the cup in a small circle while telling a story.
Lift the cup to reveal the surprise position.
missions.secret: Preparation and matching colors guide where the audience looks.
missions.showTip: This one is about acting. Look where you want the audience to look.

before: Is a grown-up nearby?
Are coins away from younger kids?
Does your paper match the table?
during: Can you move the cup smoothly?
Are you looking at the right place?

missions.title: The Rising Ring
missions.subtitle: Make a ring climb a rubber band.
safety: Do not snap the rubber band at people.
Keep small rings away from younger children.
missions.effect: A ring climbs up a slanted rubber band by itself.
steps: Thread the ring onto the rubber band.
Hold the rubber band so it slopes upward.
Hide extra slack in the lower hand.
Slowly release the hidden slack.
The ring appears to climb upward.
missions.secret: The ring is really sliding along hidden slack as you release it.
missions.showTip: Move slowly. Say, 'Climb, little ring,' and keep your lower hand relaxed.

before: Do you have a rubber band and ring?
Can you hold the band without snapping it?
during: Did you hide extra slack?
Can you release the slack slowly?

missions.title: The Can That Follows You
missions.subtitle: Move an empty can without touching it.
safety: Use an empty clean can with no sharp edges.
Do not use a broken or crushed can.
Balloons are not for younger children.
missions.effect: An empty can rolls toward a comb or balloon without being touched.
steps: Place an empty can on its side on a smooth table or floor.
Rub a plastic comb on dry hair or cloth.
Hold the comb near the can without touching it.
Slowly move the comb away.
The can follows.
missions.secret: Static electricity pulls lightly on the can.
missions.showTip: Act like the comb is a tiny magnet wand, but explain the science secret after the show.

before: Is the can clean and safe?
Is the surface smooth?
Is a grown-up nearby?
during: Did you charge the comb by rubbing it?
Are you close but not touching?

missions.title: Prediction Star
missions.subtitle: Predict the answer before the math begins.
safety: No physical risk. Keep pencils away from faces.
missions.effect: You write a prediction first, and the helper's final answer matches it.
steps: Secretly write the number 1 on a paper and fold it.
Ask a helper to pick any number.
Tell them: add 4, double it, subtract 6, divide by 2, subtract the starting number.
Ask for the final answer.
Open your prediction: 1.
missions.secret: The math cancels their starting number and always leaves 1.
missions.showTip: Make the folded prediction feel important. Put it under a cup before the trick starts.

before: Did you write the prediction before starting?
Can your helper do the math?
during: Did they add 4?
Did they double it?
Did they subtract the starting number at the end?
after: Want to learn how to make your own math spell?
